mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals improving household demand and revenue expectations, raising S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; lower or negative sales momentum predicts weaker returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 427186.0 for 2014-02-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the rolling 3-year walk-forward Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 or negative in more than half of evaluated windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing lower expected earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after the labor deterioration is observed.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months or crosses above its 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 6.7 for 2014-02-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce nonnegative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time and the mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When the policy rate is pinned near zero and CPI inflation is not accelerating, discount-rate pressure remains low without an inflation shock, supporting higher equity valuations and positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 0.07 for 2014-02-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns during triggered low-rate low-inflation observations are not higher than baseline returns or have a negative mean in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production signals weakening goods output and operating leverage, reducing earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was 101.0251 for 2014-01-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases and no negative average return differential versus untriggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in housing starts reflects stronger credit-sensitive demand and future construction activity, improving cyclically exposed earnings and raising S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 880.0 for 2014-01-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if 6-month HOUST growth has a walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the top tercile of HOUST growth underperforms the bottom tercile.

mechanism_narrative: Very strong payroll growth combined with a rising policy rate signals overheating and tightening risk, increasing discount rates and wage-margin pressure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month gain exceeds 600 thousand while FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 137699.0 for 2014-02-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered overheating-tightening observations have average 60-day S&P 500 returns at or above the unconditional average in at least two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden CPI inflation acceleration raises expected policy tightening and compresses equity valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the inflation signal is observed.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 234.933 for 2014-01-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered CPI acceleration episodes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more than 60% of the time and the mean 5-day return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth paired with falling unemployment confirms broad demand expansion, raising earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 two-quarter growth is positive and UNRATE falls at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 15932.9 for 2013-10-01 in the available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered broad-expansion observations fail to outperform unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns or produce a negative hit-rate differential versus nontriggered observations.
